evidence: We use artificial intelligence and machine learning technology (collectively, AI) capabilities with the goal of enhancing efficiencies in conducting our business.
We have implemented guidelines and policies specifically governing the use of AI tools in the workplace.
Our deployment and application of AI remains ongoing. While these AI tools hold promise in optimizing our work processes and driving efficiencies...
We use artificial intelligence and machine learning technology capabilities with the goal of enhancing efficiencies in conducting our business.
the release, leak or disclosure of proprietary, confidential, sensitive or otherwise valuable information as a result of or in connection with our use of AI tools

the production of incomplete, inaccurate or otherwise flawed outputs...reliance on such outputs which could result in...reputational and competitive harm, customer loss, legal liability, errors in our decision-making
the evolving legal regulations relating to AI, which may require significant resources to modify and maintain business practices to comply with applicable law

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts, headcount, or named AI programs disclosed; only generic efficiency-enhancement language and policy implementation mentioned.
ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use described as 'ongoing' with no named initiatives, deployed use cases, or quantified outcomes; language is largely aspirational.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk scenarios are company-specific with named mechanisms (data leakage, flawed outputs, regulatory compliance costs, reputational harm), but no quantification or named governance body beyond 'guidelines and policies'; scored 4 rather than 5 due to absence of quantified risk treatment or formal oversight structure.
